chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitutional Petition No.S-257 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For orders on M.A No.4160/2025. 3. For hearing of main case. 18.02.2026 Mr. Mir Pervaiz Akhtar Talpur, advocate for petitioners. Mr. Irfan Umrani, advocate for respondent No.1(a). = Matter was heard at length. Per learned counsel for the petitioners, the suit was decreed against the petitioners, which has been assailed before the learned Additional District Judge, Khipro, in Civil Appeal No.01/2025, which is pending adjudication. In the meanwhile, Execution Application No.02/2025 was allowed by the learned Executing Court based upon the report of the bailiff dated 12.12.2025, on which date possession was handed over to the decree holder. Learned counsel has drawn attention to the order dated 10.12.2025 passed in this petition whereby a status quo order was passed upon the statement of the petitioner that the parties are going to patch up their differences out of court. The instant petition has been filed against the impugned order dated 24.11.2025 whereby Civil Revision Application No.Nil/2025 was dismissed as being barred by limitation of 29 days beyond the prescribed period. In view of the fact that the execution application has already been allowed and, as per the bailiff’s report, possession of the subject property has already been handed over to the respondent/decree holder, both the learned counsel state that they would be satisfied if the petition is disposed of in the terms that the petitioners be allowed to agitate their 2 grievances before the learned Additional District Judge, Khipro, in Civil Appeal No.01/2025.

The instant petition has been filed against the impugned order dated 24.11.2025 whereby Civil Revision Application No.Nil/2025 was dismissed as being barred by limitation of 29 days beyond the prescribed period. In view of the fact that the execution application has already been allowed and, as per the bailiff’s report, possession of the subject property has already been handed over to the respondent/decree holder, both the learned counsel state that they would be satisfied if the petition is disposed of in the terms that the petitioners be allowed to agitate their 2 grievances before the learned Additional District Judge, Khipro, in Civil Appeal No.01/2025. Order accordingly. The instant petition is disposed of alongwith listed application by consent of the parties in above terms whereby the Civil Appeal shall be decided strictly in accordance with law. JUDGE *Faisal *
